lesson.menu_desc: Emergencies, the phone, and important numbers -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Calling for Help
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marlene Landucci | Aula 19 -- Calling for Help

listenings.text: Emergency services, how can I help you? Please stay calm and speak slowly. First, tell me what happened. Is it a fire, an accident, or a medical problem? Next, tell me where you are. Give me the name of the street and the number of the building. Then, tell me if someone is hurt. If a person is hurt, do not move them. Help is on the way. We will send the police, an ambulance, or the fire department. Please stay on the phone with me. Do not hang up. Tell me your name and a phone number. Everything will be okay. Help is coming now.

Hi Marlene, this is the hotel reception, Mister Costa. I am calling to help you. If you have an emergency in your room, please call number nine on the phone. For a fire, leave the room and use the stairs, not the elevator. For a medical problem, we can call an ambulance for you. The number for the police is right next to the phone. There is also a small first-aid box in the bathroom. If you feel in danger, call me any time, day or night. Please save my number. Stay safe, and have a good evening.

extra_audio.text: First, there is an emergency and you feel in danger. Then you pick up the phone and call for help. After that, you tell the operator what happened. Next, you give your address and say if someone is hurt. Finally, you stay calm and wait for the police or the ambulance.
